review: 1.10.0-RC4c mesh hot-path robustness — adversarial implementation review before cut
method: 3 lenses (transport-correctness, wiring-gating, spec-fidelity-regression) + skeptic verify + synthesis; 4 agents

verdict.note: All load-bearing claims verified against the tree: BL5 snapshot (no per-message Redis on broadcast; unicast warm-fallback), hot-path-only backoff (tick dials raw), WRITER_IDLE reap, BL4 reconcile at sender-start end, BL2 guard — all correct. mesh.enable=false / interest-routing.enable=false byte-identical paths untouched. Full reactor 627 → 629 tests / 11 modules / 0 skips green.

findings_allMinor_FOLDED_before_cut.title: MeshIdleReapTest omitted the spec §6 'next send re-dials and delivers' assertion (3 overlapping findings)
findings_allMinor_FOLDED_before_cut.fix: extended MeshIdleReapTest: after the reap, a.connectionTo re-dials a fresh active channel that is re-cached

findings_allMinor_FOLDED_before_cut.title: Unknown-target unicast drop+count path untested (MeshBroker.unicast addr==null branch)
Backoff-recovery (round-1 MAJOR resolution) tested only in decomposed halves, not end-to-end through membershipTick
findings_allMinor_FOLDED_before_cut.fix: MeshReconnectBackoffTest.tickRecoversDegradedNode_despiteSendPathBackoff: DEGRADED + send-path backoff → peer recovers → one raw tick restores ACTIVE

findings_allMinor_FOLDED_before_cut.title: Production robustness nit (bounded, at-most-once-preserved, ~unreachable under default timings): a stale future-dated reconnect entry could briefly suppress a send to a tick-recovered + idle-reaped peer
findings_allMinor_FOLDED_before_cut.fix: one-liner: reconnect.remove(peerNodeId) in connectionForSend's cached-active fast path (a live channel ⇒ peer reachable ⇒ clear stale backoff)

postFold: Folded tests green (MeshIdleReap 1, MeshSnapshot 3, MeshReconnectBackoff 2); production one-liner clears stale backoff on a cached-active channel. All 6 findings folded into the cut rather than deferred (project fold-before-cut culture).
